channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 8 hours.

item.title: 🛡️ Austrian jets intercept unauthorized US military planes two days in a row | Defense News
item.description: The May 10 intercept reportedly took place over the Totes Gebirge mountain range in Upper Austria, over 60 kilometers from the German border.

item.title: 🏴 In Latvia, military robots roll across a new communication challenge: woodlands | Breaking Defense
item.description: Several users reported significant challenges with the UGVs’ communication systems, many of them using Starlink, in densely forested areas of the Baltic nation.

item.title: 🌏 The Missing Navies: The Hormuz Crisis and the Limits of America’s Indo-Pacific Partnerships | War on the Rocks
item.description: On May 4, 2026, a South Korean vessel came under fire in the Strait of Hormuz, leading President Donald Trump to urge the government in Seoul to join the U.S.-led operation to secure the waterway. The South Korean government politely replied it would “review” the American…

item.title: 🏛️ Dazed and Confused: How Marine Corps Promotion Boards Keep Getting It Wrong | War on the Rocks
item.description: An old adage claims a marine’s career came down to the “two or three minutes of a promotions brief.” Turns out that was a little optimistic. In practice, the Marine Corps promotion system decides the institutional worth of a 20‑year career in about 12 minutes of board…

item.title: ⚛️ Pakistan’s New Logic of Limited War May Not Keep War Limited | War on the Rocks
item.description: In May 2025, as Indian BrahMos missile strikes hit Pakistani air bases — targeting runways, parked aircraft, and critical infrastructure — Islamabad faced a choice. It had the capability to respond with its own long-range systems, but it chose not to. Pakistan deliberately…

item.title: 🌏 No solidarity: Southeast Asian countries split as each seeks Iranian oil | ASPI Strategist
item.description: Southeast Asian governments are failing to respond as a bloc to the severe disruption in oil, gas and commercial shipments through the Strait of Hormuz. Malaysia, Thailand, the Philippines and Vietnam have each moved to ...

item.title: 📍 India’s Role in a Disordered World | Foreign Policy
item.description: Diplomacy of the highest quality is more essential than ever.

item.title: 🌏 America Has Lost Its Leverage Over China | Foreign Affairs
item.description: How Trump and Xi could cement Beijing’s advantage for years to come.

item.title: 🌏 The Promise and Peril of U.S.-China Summitry | Foreign Affairs
item.description: What Xi wants from Trump—and Trump might get from Xi.

item.title: 📍 2026 defence budget – A$17.4 billion in spending uplift still undecided | ASPI Strategist
item.description: About A$17.4 billion of the Australian government’s promised A$53 billion increase in headline defence spending over the next 10 years remains uncertain. Of the uplift promised by the National Defence Strategy (NDS) a month ago ...

item.title: 🏛️ In reversal, Hegseth now backs E-7 radar plane | Defense One
item.description: SecDef says previous calls to cancel the program reflected a now-discarded ”divest-to-invest mindset.”

item.title: 🏛️ Drone boats make debut in Navy’s 30-year shipbuilding plan | Defense One
item.description: Service officials envision a 450-hull fleet by 2031.

item.title: 🏛️ Pentagon’s Mindset On E-7 Radar Aircraft It Tried To Axe Has Completely Changed: Hegseth | The War Zone
item.description: E-7s to replace the Air Force's dwindling and aging fleet of E-3s are even more sorely needed now after one of the latter was lost to an Iranian attack. The post Pentagon’s Mindset On E-7 Radar Aircraft It Tried To Axe Has Completely Changed: Hegseth appeared first on The War…

item.title: ⚛️ Trump’s Golden Dome missile shield estimated to cost $1.2 trillion | Defense News
item.description: A national missile defense system would cost $1.2 trillion to build and maintain over the next 20 years, a nonpartisan federal agency estimated.

item.title: 🏴 Russia Plans To Deploy Sarmat ICBM Operationally Later This Year | The War Zone
item.description: The news came after Moscow claimed it successfully tested Sarmat, which has been beset by technical issues and delays. The post Russia Plans To Deploy Sarmat ICBM Operationally Later This Year appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: ⚛️ Trump-class Battleship will get same nuclear reactor as Ford aircraft carrier | Breaking Defense
item.description: Chief of Naval Operations Adm. Daryl Caudle confirmed the battleship would share several design features with the USS Ford.

item.title: 📍 The Geopolitics of the Trump-Xi Meeting | The Diplomat
item.description: Amid the expected pomp and circumstance, what can we expect from the Trump-Xi summit?

item.title: 🌏 What Happened to Trump the China Hawk? | Foreign Policy
item.description: The U.S. president heads to Beijing in detente and dealmaking mode.

item.title: 📍 How Long Can Keir Starmer Hang On? | Foreign Policy
item.description: The British prime minister is barely clinging to power over a split and unpopular Labour Party.

item.title: 🏴 Ukrainian long-range drones are turning Russia’s size into a weakness | Atlantic Council
item.description: Ukraine is waging a strategic bombing campaign of deep strikes across the Russian Federation that aims to exploit Russia's colossal size and transform it from a key strength into a fatal weakness, writes Peter Dickinson. The post Ukrainian long-range drones are turning Russia’s…

item.title: 🏛️ Hegseth rethinking Army reforms, cuts to aviation | Defense One
item.description: One year after launching the Army Transformation Initiative, SecDef says he's reviewing the to-do list.

item.title: 🕵️ US, close allies creating joint ‘orbital warfare’ plan: SPACECOM chief | Breaking Defense
item.description: Gen. Stephen Whiting told the Mitchell Institute today that SPACECOM also plans to Apollo Insights table-top exercises (TTXs) with commercial industry once a quarter this year.

item.title: 🏛️ Hegseth: Pentagon is ‘taking another look’ at deep cuts to Army’s aircraft budget | Breaking Defense
item.description: Lawmakers expressed concern over the Army’s proposed budget, which takes an ax to high-profile legacy helicopter procurement.

item.title: 🏛️ Defense contractors have donated millions to members of Congress, new report finds | Defense News
item.description: The defense industry donations make up only a small percentage of the cash going to Congress, however, according to OpenSecrets.

item.title: 🌏 What Trump and Xi Want From Their Summit | Foreign Policy
item.description: The leaders are expected to talk trade, Taiwan, and the Iran war.

item.title: 🏴 EU targets Kyrgyzstan as Brussels seeks to prevent Russian sanctions evasion | Atlantic Council
item.description: The latest EU sanctions on Russia also featured measures against Kyrgyzstan. This was the first use of so-called anti-circumvention tools, which are designed to prevent third countries from helping the Kremlin bypass restrictions over the invasion of Ukraine, writes Marc…

item.title: 🏴 Drone-Hunting M28 Skytruck In The Works For Poland | The War Zone
item.description: With the Ukrainian An-28 reportedly highly effective in the role, Poland is converting its similar M28 for the counter-drone mission. The post Drone-Hunting M28 Skytruck In The Works For Poland appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 📍 The missing link in the US energy advantage: Connecting supply to demand | Atlantic Council
item.description: The US has the energy supplies to help meet rising demand—its challenge is building the infrastructure to connect one to the other. Permitting reform could change this. The post The missing link in the US energy advantage: Connecting supply to demand appeared first on Atlantic…

item.title: 🏴 Something else for Australia to consider: Russian nuclear subs in the Pacific | ASPI Strategist
item.description: Strategists in Canberra should factor in the plausible risk of Russian participation in a Western Pacific conflict, especially action by Russian submarines. Russia has modernised its Pacific submarines and has a clear reason for using ...

item.title: 🏛️ IRGC Navy Claims Vast Expansion In Its Definition Of Strait Of Hormuz (Updated) | The War Zone
item.description: It remains unclear how the IRGC will enforce its tenfold increase in what it considers the Strait. The post IRGC Navy Claims Vast Expansion In Its Definition Of Strait Of Hormuz (Updated) appeared first on The War Zone.
